concepts.name: Inflation
concepts.definition: The general increase in prices of goods and services over time, which reduces the purchasing power of money.

concepts.name: Consumer Price Index (CPI)
concepts.definition: A measure of inflation that tracks price changes of a fixed basket of goods and services typically purchased by households, reflecting the cost of living for consumers.

concepts.name: GDP Deflator
concepts.definition: A measure of inflation reflecting price changes for all goods and services produced domestically, using a changing basket that reflects the current production mix.

concepts.name: Household Budgets
concepts.definition: The allocation of income by households to goods and services, which is affected by inflation through changes in purchasing power.

concepts.name: Fixed Income
concepts.definition: Household income that does not adjust with inflation, such as pensions, making purchasing power vulnerable to inflation.

concepts.name: Variable Income
concepts.definition: Household income that adjusts with inflation, allowing maintenance of purchasing power despite rising prices.

concepts.name: Policy Communication
concepts.definition: The process by which policymakers explain inflation causes, impacts, and control measures to the public to foster understanding and trust.

processes.name: Calculating CPI Inflation
steps: Determine the cost of a fixed basket of goods in the base year.
Determine the cost of the same basket in the current year.
Calculate the inflation rate as ((current cost - base cost) / base cost) × 100.

processes.name: Calculating GDP Deflator
steps: Obtain nominal GDP for the current period.
Obtain real GDP for the current period.
Calculate the GDP deflator as (nominal GDP / real GDP) × 100.

processes.name: Policy Communication on Inflation
steps: Explain causes of inflation to the public.
Describe impacts of inflation on households and the economy.
Announce measures taken to control inflation, such as interest rate adjustments.
Provide transparency to help households and businesses plan.

misconceptions.name: CPI and GDP Deflator Measure the Same Inflation
misconceptions.description: A common misconception is that CPI and GDP deflator measure inflation identically, whereas CPI uses a fixed basket of consumer goods and GDP deflator uses a variable basket of all domestically produced goods and services.
